seoTitle: Saltwater vs San Remo — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Saltwater and San Remo. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Saltwater vs San Remo: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Saltwater and San Remo.
heading: Saltwater vs San Remo — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Saltwater, New South Wales and San Remo, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: San Remo has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Saltwater (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Saltwater or San Remo safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Saltwater has a flood risk score of 25/100, while San Remo scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Saltwater scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. San Remo scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Saltwater or San Remo?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. San Remo has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
